title: Indus Waters Treaty fallout? Nearly a third of Pakistan reels as Sindh, Balochistan face water crisis
summary_full: Pakistan grapples with a severe water shortage affecting one-third of its population. Last year, India suspended the Indus Waters Treaty after terror attacks. This has intensified water scarcity in Sindh and Balochistan. Irrigation canals face significant deficits. Allegations of unequal water distribution fuel political disputes. Farmers struggle as cultivation is hampered. The crisis highlights vulnerabilities in Pakistan's water management.
